message:
test(indexer): table-driven severity routing for every cleanup disposition (bd-yv5fn)

Peer commit 7d3297c7 added the ibuuh.19 structured-logs emission in
\`LexicalCleanupDryRunPlan::record_inventory\` (eight dispositions
routed across three severity tiers: two \`DEBUG\`, four \`INFO\`,
two \`WARN\`), and my follow-up 7e71b27f added a regression test —
but that test covers only \`QuarantinedRetained\`. A future refactor
that re-routes any OTHER variant to the wrong tier, or drops
emission entirely for one specific variant, would stay green
against that test.

Add a table-driven companion
\`record_inventory_emits_correct_severity_for_every_disposition_variant\`
that exercises all eight variants in one pass.

Test design:

- One local \`ClassificationCollector\` layer plus
  \`StringVisitor\` (same shape as the existing sibling test) so
  captured events arrive as \`(level, target, fields)\` triples
  regardless of which tier fired. Installed once via
  \`tracing::subscriber::with_default\` and shared across all
  cases.

- Fixture shapes follow the real
  \`classify_generation_for_cleanup\` branch decisions at
  \`src/indexer/lexical_generation.rs:~1522\`:
  - \`CurrentPublished\`: build=Validated, publish=Published,
    single Validated shard so \`is_serveable\` returns true.
  - \`ActiveWork\`: default \`new_scratch\` state.
  - \`QuarantinedRetained\`: publish=Quarantined (kept for
    completeness so the table is exhaustive).
  - \`SupersededReclaimable\`: publish=Superseded with a
    Planned shard carrying \`artifact_bytes > 0\` so
    reclaimable bytes are non-zero.
  - \`SupersededRetained\`: publish=Superseded with a
    Published (reclaimable=false) shard, no reclaim bytes, so
    the classifier picks the retained arm.
  - \`FailedReclaimable\`: build=Failed with an Abandoned
    shard (reclaimable=true) and \`artifact_bytes > 0\`.
  - \`FailedRetained\`: build=Failed with a Published
    (pinned=true) shard so reclaim is blocked.
  - \`PinnedRetained\`: build=Validated, publish=Staged, zero
    shards — classifier falls through to the pinned arm.

- Runs each fixture through
  \`LexicalCleanupDryRunPlan::from_manifests(...)\` under the
  collector, then walks the captured events and asserts:
  - exactly one event per record_inventory call,
  - the event target is \`cass::indexer::lexical_cleanup\`,
  - the event level matches the severity table in 7d3297c7
    (DEBUG for \`SupersededReclaimable\` /
    \`FailedReclaimable\`; INFO for \`ActiveWork\`,
    \`CurrentPublished\`, \`SupersededRetained\`,
    \`PinnedRetained\`; WARN for \`QuarantinedRetained\` /
    \`FailedRetained\`),
  - \`disposition\`, \`generation_id\`, \`reason\`, and the
    per-variant byte-count field expected by operators are all
    populated.

Pure test addition. No production code or public API touched.
